title: NGL (NGL) Q4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
author: Motley Fool Transcribing
description: NGL Energy Partners LP reported strong Q4 2026 results with record Water Solutions segment performance, achieving $660 million adjusted EBITDA for the year at the high end of guidance. The company completed a $950 million refinancing, redeemed 47% of Class D preferred units, and repurchased 8.7 million common units. Management guided 2027 consolidated adjusted EBITDA to $715-$725 million (approximately 10% year-over-year growth), driven by Water Solutions expansion including the LEX II system capacity increase to 560,000 barrels per day, with potential expansion to 650,000 barrels pending customer commitments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company delivered record Water Solutions performance with 11% year-over-year EBITDA growth, achieved high-end guidance, successfully executed balance sheet restructuring (47% preferred unit redemption), improved operational efficiency (operating costs down to $0.22/barrel), secured long-term contracted volumes (53% of disposal volumes under commitments), and provided positive 2027 guidance of up to 10% growth. Strong demand indicators with management noting 'line out the door' for additional capacity.

title: Natural Gas Prices Surge As Arctic Blast Freezes US: 3 Stocks On The Move
author: Piero Cingari
description: Natural gas prices have surged due to an arctic blast sweeping across the US, driving up heating demand. This has led to gains in several natural gas-related stocks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions that NGL Energy Partners' stock rose 1.2% to $5.05, buoyed by expectations of increased activity in the natural gas infrastructure sector due to the surge in natural gas prices.
